claim: When the active domain has no content yet (missing graph), `hotam gen-spec` shall emit a 'no content yet' notice into docs/gen/*.md, not fail.
why: Atom of R-empty-content-is-legitimate (gen-notice concern). D2 split decided by domain-user 2026-06-30. WHY: failing on missing content would block the regen step of the closed loop for new adopters. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: genSpec (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go) now loads the domain graph via loadGraphOrEmpty, which detects os.IsNotExist on the missing graph.json specifically and substitutes an empty graph (&ontology.Graph{DomainDir: domainDir}) instead of propagating the raw read error. An empty graph flows through every generator unchanged: the Build* helpers in internal/generator already detect g.IsEmpty() and render the calm EmptyNotice (internal/generator/common.go) into docs/gen/*.md, so the missing-file case produces output identical to the empty-but-present case (the two situations are indistinguishable to an adopter with nothing modeled yet). The discrimination is mechanical and the only one that should trigger the calm path: errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) — a genuine error (a malformed graph.json decode failure, or a permissions error) still surfaces as a real error, enforced by the TestGenSpec_MissingGraph_MalformedStillErrors non-vacuity control. The identical missing-file gap also exists in the what-now code path (R-empty-content-calm-banner's own why documents this); that is NOT this requirement's anchor and remains an honest open gap there — loadGraphOrEmpty is deliberately scoped to gen-spec, not shared via loadDomainGraph, to avoid expanding scope across every loader caller.
evidence: cmd/hotam/gen_spec_test.go::TestGenSpec_MissingGraphRendersCalmNotice — genSpec against a temp dir containing NO graph.json returns no error and renders the calm 'No domain content loaded' notice into docs/gen/REQUIREMENTS.md, mirroring the empty-but-present case. TestGenSpec_MissingGraph_MalformedStillErrors is the non-vacuity control: a malformed graph.json (decode error, not os.IsNotExist) still propagates as a real error, proving the calm path is specific to the missing-file case.
